title: Sector Performance Drivers: Why Mega-Cap Exposure Matters
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Mega-cap companies significantly influence sector performance, but ETFs often underweight them due to regulatory caps, creating a gap in sector exposure. BlackR
excerpt: Mega-cap companies significantly influence sector performance, but ETFs often underweight them due to regulatory caps, creating a gap in sector exposure. BlackRock's TOPT ETF helps close this gap, aligning exposure more closely with benchmarks. This underweighting can mislead investors about their t

content.introduction: Mega-cap companies significantly influence sector performance, but ETFs often underweight them due to regulatory caps, creating a gap in sector exposure. BlackRock's TOPT ETF helps close this gap, aligning exposure more closely with benchmarks. This underweighting can mislead investors about their true sector holdings.
body: Mega-cap companies drive sector returns, but traditional sector ETFs may not fully capture this exposure due to regulatory diversification
While price appreciation for mega-cap tech stocks has a fundamental driver in earnings growth, it has also resulted in significant multiple

For example, [TOPT](https://www.blackrock.com/us/individual/products/339779/ishares-top-20-u-s-stocks-etf) can close the mega-cap gap bringing exposures closer to benchmark, while [XOEF](https://www.blackrock.com/us/individual/products/344551/ishares-s-p-500-ex-s-p-100-etf) can help [reduce mega-cap and AI exposure](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/dont-abandon-tech). Closing the mega-cap gap with TOPT:**The average asset manager (AM) moderate model is less exposed to mega-cap stocks relative to broad benchmarks like the MSCI ACWI IMI (Fig.3).14 Adding the iShares Top 20 U.S. Stocks ETF ([TOPT](https://www.blackrock.com/us/individual/products/339779/ishares-top-20-us-stocks-etf)) could close an underweight to mega-cap stocks more efficiently by requiring a smaller portfolio allocation than an S&P 500 fund used for the same purpose. The chart illustrates market-cap breakdowns (Mega, Large, Mid & Small, plus TOPT or IVV allocations).](https://www.blackrock.com/blk-one01-c-assets/cache-1777566331000/images/media-bin/web/financial-professionals/topt.svg). ](https://www.blackrock.com/blk-one01-c-assets/cache-1777566331000/images/media-bin/web/financial-professionals/charts/down-exposure-to-mega-caps2.svg). *   [Our top investment strategies](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/investments/preparing-portfolios). *   [Invest for after-tax returns](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/tax-center). *   [Investment Directions](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/inside-the-market/investment-directions). *   [Portfolio manager views](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights#explore-markets-and-portfolio-articles). *   [My Portfolios](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/tools/my-portfolios). *   [Brand & marketing](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/resources/practice-management/advisor-marketing-strategies).
